summary: Set 085 Session 3 (F4): verifier-scope audit, portable playbook, live dogfood, release prep. Audit facts: verify_session assembles the spec excerpt + git status --short + the complete diff + the up-front conventions block (test/gate outcomes ride there, mandated by promoted convention L-064-10) into the adversarial template; no process manual is fed. The constitution is deliberately not fed - a recorded deviation from the spec sentence's literal five-item list, adjudicated to the operator (see verifier_scope_adjudication). Authored docs/guidance-slimming-playbook.md (measure -> admission-test classify -> prose->gate->archive demotion -> constitution -> manifest -> ratchet; consensus cheap-mistakes limits verbatim; A/B signals). Fixed a Major portability defect found in release prep: the packaged router-config.yaml declares this repo's preload manifest, and a pip-installed consumer with no workspace config would inherit it via the bundled-default fallback (guidance_report --check hard-failing on foreign files); bundled-default resolution is now 'no config' for guidance purposes, workspace/--repo-root/AI_ROUTER_CONFIG keep enforcing; 4 new tests; documented in guidance-lifecycle.md. Releases prepared, publish pending operator authorization: router 0.30.0 (pyproject + CHANGELOG incl. rollback pinning registry-live 0.29.0) and extension 0.40.0 (package.json + CHANGELOG, template-bundle-only; rollback names registry-live 0.39.0); repository-reference rows + version walk updated. Suite: pytest 2816 passed / 5 skipped (S2 baseline + 4 guard tests); tsc clean; TS unit 1270 passing; guidance_report --check green (10,673/12,000); validate_guidance_meta OK. Verification (gpt-5-4): R1 1 Major + R2 2 Major. The R2 sequencing finding (change-log written in completed tense before disposition dogfood fields and the critique artifact existed) and both nits are remediated in this close prep (this disposition carries the dogfood record; the number reconciliation landed in change-log.md; the 'concurs' overstatement was corrected by an appended activity-log entry - the raw earlier entry stands per append-only discipline). The verifier-scope finding (I-085-S3-1) is an unfixed Major after two rounds on a genuine spec-interpretation fork - per the constitution's bounded-round rule the session STOPS TO THE OPERATOR with the adjudication packet instead of grinding a third round; status requires_review, close_session deliberately not run, notification deliberately not fired.

verifier_scope_adjudication.finding: Rounds 1-2 (gpt-5-4, Major): recording the verifier-scope audit as no-tool-change contradicts the spec sentence 'The verifier's evidence should be the diff, test output, gate outcomes, the spec, and the constitution - not the full process manual': the constitution is never assembled, and test/gate outcomes are only guaranteed when the orchestrator supplies --conventions-file. Verifier's stated correct answer: change verify_session/the template, or change the governing spec first.
verifier_scope_adjudication.orchestrator_position: No tool change, pending the operator's call. (1) Provenance: the requirement's source is the 2026-07-07 cross-provider consult - consensus-synthesis.md's adopted point scopes verifier evidence to 'diff, test output, gate outcomes, spec'; the constitution is not in the adopted list and was appended to the spec sentence at authoring time. (2) Purpose: the sentence's own parenthetical names the failure mode (a manual-fed verifier produces process-heavy critiques); feeding the constitution - a process doc - re-invites it. (3) Prefer removal over addition, and the spec's own instruction: 'if the current assembly is already scoped, record no-change-needed rather than inventing an edit'. (4) Test/gate outcomes are mandated into every session-verification prompt by promoted convention L-064-10 (the up-front conventions block); hard-requiring the CLI flag would add a second enforcement surface for an already-mandated practice (a named anti-pattern of this set). Every 085 round actually carried them.
verifier_scope_adjudication.verifier_context: The verifier saw the spec excerpt (with the literal five-item sentence), the diff, and the conventions block (which presented the provenance argument in round 2). It weighed the spec's literal text as the contract and the absence of a spec change in the diff as dispositive; it did not treat the consensus-synthesis provenance (an artifact outside the evidence bundle) as authoritative over the spec text - a defensible reading of its instructions.
verifier_scope_adjudication.self_assessment: The round-1 nit was correct and is fixed: earlier S3 artifacts summarized the internally-split routed second opinion (s3-verifier-scope-audit.txt: top-line 'Unscoped - add the constitution'; reasoned answer 2 'omission is the better state; amend the spec allow-list') as 'concurs'. On the substance, confidence is high that the consensus-adopted scope list is the real contract and constitution-feeding would be a regression against this set's thesis - but the canonical wording of the spec sentence is an operator decision encoded at authoring time, so amending it (or overriding it) is the operator's call, not the orchestrator's.
operator_options: ACCEPT the deviation: affirm the consensus scope list (diff, test output, gate outcomes, spec); optionally amend the spec sentence / record the affirmation in the Step 9 review; re-verify round 3 with the affirmation stated as an operator decision, or attest the verdict via the operator-adjudicated path.
MANDATE the literal list: direct a follow-up change to verify_session (assemble docs/session-constitution.md into the bundle; optionally make the conventions block a hard requirement) and re-verify.
RE-VERIFY RESHAPED: same evidence, with the adjudication framing presented as settled operator context.
SECOND OPINION: route the same question to a third provider before deciding.

path_aware_critique_adjudication.artifact: path-aware-critique.json (pull_critique, providers google gemini-2.5-pro + openai gpt-5.4, diff-base 5ce3b3a, run after verification round 2)
path_aware_critique_adjudication.convergence: No finding drives a new code change -> converged per the iterative-dogfood convention (L-070-1); the final-round artifact is the gate artifact.

findings.critic: openai gpt-5.4 #1 (Major: path-aware-critique.json missing at the session-set root)
findings.adjudication: resolved-by-construction / false-positive: the critique necessarily reviews the repo state before its own artifact exists; the very run that produced this finding wrote the artifact it reports missing. The close gate validates presence at set-terminal close.

findings.critic: openai gpt-5.4 #2 (Major: verifier evidence omits the constitution / conventions block optional, vs the spec sentence's literal list)
findings.adjudication: duplicate of I-085-S3-1 - the same spec-interpretation fork the verification rounds surfaced, independently corroborated on the path-aware surface; pending-operator per verifier_scope_adjudication above. No code change pending that call.
clean - zero findings; explicitly verifies the bundled-default guard, the fail-closed --check matrix, path containment, back-compat, and the test suite against the docs and changelog.

dogfood.description: Set 085 S3 Step 3 live-dogfood datapoint: this session ran from the slimmed preload only, on the operator's claude/anthropic/claude-fable-5/high seat.

on_demand_opens.trigger: S3.2 playbook authoring needed the canonical admission-test and manifest wording; also consulted to document the bundled-default guard
set-terminal required critique stage mechanics; the workflow doc was never opened for the happy path

dogfood.time_to_first_task_action_note: measured from start_session registration to the first S3.1 evidence read; the ~4 minutes included Step 3 prereq checks and the Step 3.5 ai-assignment block + routed next-set analysis
dogfood.verification_rounds_note: R1 1 Major, R2 2 Major; loop stopped at the bounded-round rule on a spec-interpretation fork (adjudication above) rather than ground further - contrast S1's 12 rounds under the pre-slimming corpus
dogfood.gate_failures_note: guidance_report --check, pytest, tsc, and TS unit all green on first post-change run; no close gate reached this session (close deliberately deferred to the operator adjudication)
